name: availability: a pre-B7 booking with blank dates holds a room link but blocks nothing (B8/F16)
comment: The locked migration decision: a booking with no Check In/Check Out has no range to compare, so it takes no part in the overlap check even though it carries a Room link. recB0 is shaped exactly like the 11 legacy records live in the base today — Room linked, dates blank. The guest is accepted and gets that same room. Stated plainly because it is a real property of the system, not an accident: any date-less row (a legacy record, or a Walk-in typed into Airtable by hand) is invisible to availability and cannot reserve anything.

includes: New booking enquiry from John Smith
How many of you are staying
